varieties.name: Natchez
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1978, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Tall upright tree 6-9m, pure white flowers, large panicles 20-30cm
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate. High powdery mildew resistance. Heat tolerant. Attractive cinnamon-brown exfoliating bark. Good autumn color (bronze-red). Vigorous growth suited to humid conditions.
varieties.availability: Widely available: Speciality Trees Australia, Fleming's Nurseries, Evergreen Trees Direct, Australian Plants Online, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Tuscarora
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1967, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree 5-6m, coral-pink to rose-red flowers, upright form
varieties.growing_notes: Performs excellently in Brisbane humidity. Powdery mildew resistant. Heat and humidity tolerant. Stunning reddish-orange fall foliage. Exfoliating bark adds winter interest.
varieties.availability: Speciality Trees, Fleming's Nurseries (Indian Summer series), Burke's Backyard retailers

varieties.name: Sioux
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1983, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree 4-5m, bright carmine-pink/musk pink flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited to subtropical Brisbane. Highly mildew resistant. Handles heat and humidity well. Rich yellow, orange, red fall colors. Caramel-pink mottled bark when mature.
varieties.availability: Fleming's Nurseries, Speciality Trees, Online Plants Australia, Brisbane garden centers

varieties.name: Lipan
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree 4-5m, compact growth habit, lavender-mauve flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts well to Brisbane subtropical conditions. Mildew resistant. Compact form ideal for smaller Brisbane gardens. Yellow-orange fall foliage. Dark green disease-resistant leaves.
varieties.availability: Speciality Trees Australia, Fleming's Nurseries, Queensland retail nurseries

varieties.name: Zuni
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1979, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Small tree/large shrub 3-4m, violet-purple flowers, can be multi-stem
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane climate. Powdery mildew resistant. Handles subtropical humidity. Foliage has red tinges when young, orange-red-maroon fall display. Peeling mottled bark.
varieties.availability: Fleming's Nurseries (Indian Summer range), Speciality Trees, Brisbane nurseries

varieties.name: Tonto
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Compact 3m height, rich pink to fuchsia-red flowers, dense form
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane suburban gardens. Mildew resistant. Compact size suits smaller yards. Heat and humidity tolerant. Good for hedging or specimen.
varieties.availability: Fleming's Nurseries, Burke's Backyard stockists, Evergreen Trees Direct, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Yuma
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree 4m, pale lavender-pink flowers, upright form
varieties.growing_notes: Grows well in Brisbane subtropical climate. Some mildew resistance. Softer color palette for cooler garden schemes. Handles heat adequately.
varieties.availability: Speciality Trees, Queensland garden centers, Burke's Backyard retailers

varieties.name: Acoma
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1986, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Small tree 3m, white flowers, weeping/spreading habit
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable with unique weeping form. Mildew resistant. Attractive spreading canopy. White flowers cool in summer heat. Good for feature planting.
varieties.availability: Fleming's Nurseries (Indian Summer series), Speciality Trees, Australian retail nurseries

varieties.name: Biloxi
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Large tree form, pink flowers, single trunk training
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane as larger specimen. Mildew resistant. Can be trained to single trunk. Orange autumn foliage. Handles subtropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Speciality Trees Australia, larger Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Muskogee
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Large tree 6-9m, light lavender flowers, upright vase shape
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in Brisbane heat and humidity. Highly mildew resistant. Makes excellent shade/street tree. Beautiful exfoliating bark. Red-orange fall color.
varieties.availability: Major Queensland nurseries, Fleming's Nurseries, Speciality Trees

varieties.name: Pocomoke
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, 1998, dwarf cultivar
varieties.traits: True dwarf 0.6-1.5m tall and wide, compact mounded form
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific for dwarf variety, mid-summer to fall
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane container gardens and small spaces. Mildew resistant. Excellent for subtropical patios. Glossy green foliage turns bronze-red in fall. Hardy to Brisbane winters.
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, online retailers, Brisbane garden centers

varieties.name: Chickasaw
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, dwarf cultivar
varieties.traits: Dwarf 0.6-1m, very compact, pink flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable for borders and containers. Disease resistant. Ideal for small subtropical gardens. Low maintenance.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may be harder to find in Australia

varieties.name: Magic Series (various)
varieties.origin: Fleming's Nurseries Australia, semi-dwarf breeding
varieties.traits: Compact 1-2m, various flower colors, some with purple foliage

varieties.name: Lagerstroemia indica (species)
varieties.origin: China and Korea, introduced to cultivation c.1790
varieties.traits: Variable 2-8m shrub/small tree, multiple stems
varieties.growing_notes: HEIRLOOM SPECIES - 200+ years of cultivation. Grows well in Brisbane but more susceptible to powdery mildew than hybrids. Still grown for traditional gardens. Deciduous in Brisbane winter.
varieties.availability: Seed-grown plants at some nurseries, heritage plant specialists

varieties.name: Lagerstroemia archeriana
varieties.origin: INDIGENOUS to northern Australia, naturally occurs in Queensland
varieties.traits: Small tree 5-7m, multi-trunk, open canopy
varieties.growing_notes: AUSTRALIAN NATIVE - perfect for Brisbane climate. Naturally adapted to subtropical conditions. Deciduous late winter. Orange-red fall colors. Seed pods attract cockatoos. Excellent for native gardens.
varieties.availability: Paten Park Native Nursery, Burringbar Rainforest Nursery, Australian native specialists, Brisbane native plant nurseries

varieties.name: Little Chief (Pink)
varieties.origin: Australia, dwarf breeding
varieties.traits: Dwarf 1-1.5m, pink flowers, compact shrub form
varieties.growing_notes: Queensland favorite for small suburban gardens. Suited to Brisbane heat. Compact growth ideal for courtyard gardens.
varieties.availability: Queensland Gardening Pages nurseries, Brisbane retailers

varieties.name: Dynamite
varieties.origin: USA, Dr. Carl Whitcomb breeding, 1990s
varieties.traits: Medium 3-4m, bright red flowers, upright form
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable for bold color. Heat tolerant. Good mildew resistance. Whitcomb breeding program cultivar. Striking in subtropical gardens.
varieties.availability: Australian plant retailers, online nurseries

varieties.name: Red Rocket
varieties.origin: USA, Dr. Carl Whitcomb breeding
varieties.traits: Tall upright 6-8m, bright red flowers, rapid growth
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane as fast-growing screen or feature. Very heat tolerant. Good humidity tolerance. Red color stunning against subtropical greenery.
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, specialty growers

varieties.name: Raspberry Sundae
varieties.origin: USA, Dr. Carl Whitcomb breeding
varieties.traits: Medium 3-4m, bicolor pink and cream flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable BUT more susceptible to powdery mildew. Plant in full sun with good air circulation. Unique bicolor effect. Requires more maintenance in humid conditions.
varieties.availability: Some Australian nurseries, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Delta Jazz
varieties.origin: USA, Mississippi State University, 2010 (Southern Living Collection)
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf 2.5-3.5m, chocolate-burgundy foliage

varieties.name: Ebony Flame / Best Red
varieties.origin: USA, Dr. Cecil Pounders (USDA), Black Diamond series
varieties.traits: Medium 2.5-3.5m, deep burgundy-black foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable for high-impact subtropical landscapes. Dark foliage stable in heat. Mildew resistant. Bred from gamma-irradiated seedlings. Modern cultivar.
varieties.availability: Australian retailers, Black Diamond series stockists

varieties.name: Ebony and Ivory / Pure White
varieties.origin: USA, Dr. Cecil Pounders (USDA), Black Diamond series
varieties.traits: Medium 2.5-3.5m, burgundy-black foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical gardens. White flowers glow against dark foliage in summer heat. Disease resistant. Unique color combination.
varieties.availability: Australian plant retailers, Black Diamond series suppliers

varieties.name: Catawba
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium 3-4m, dark purple flowers, compact form

varieties.name: Cherokee
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree, bright red flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable with SOME mildew resistance. Red flower option. Subtropical adaptation adequate.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia, specialty sources

varieties.name: Seminole
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium-compact tree, pink flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable with SOME mildew resistance. Compact form. Adequate subtropical performance.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia

varieties.name: Arapaho
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree 4-6m, true red flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable. Mildew resistant. True red flower color. Handles heat and humidity well.
varieties.availability: Some Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Apalachee
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium-tall tree, lavender flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable. Mildew resistant. Lavender color option. Subtropical adaptation.
varieties.availability: Some Australian specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Potomac
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium-tall tree, pink flowers

varieties.name: Powhatan
varieties.origin: USA, National Arboretum, L. indica × L. fauriei hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium tree, purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable. Mildew resistant. Purple flower option. Subtropical performance good.
varieties.availability: Limited Australian availability

varieties.name: Fantasy
varieties.origin: USA, L. fauriei cultivar
varieties.traits: White flowers, excellent bark, tree form
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable. Based on mildew-resistant Japanese species. Attractive bark feature. Handles subtropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Specialty Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Kiowa
varieties.origin: USA, L. fauriei cultivar
varieties.traits: Large tree, white flowers, impressive size
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable for large properties. L. fauriei genetics bring excellent mildew resistance. Handles subtropical climate well.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia
